nameMeanings.name: Limbek,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Great Limber in Lindsey (Lincolnshire). This place is named with Old English lind ‘lime tree’ + beorg ‘hill’.

nameMeanings.name: til
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Cambodian Yoeum, Chheang, Noeun, Phoeun, Sambath, Soeun, Sokhom, Sombath, Sovann.  Southeast Asian Lach, Mau, Sophal, Thong, Thy, Leng, Roeun.
German, Dutch, and English: variant of   Timm  .
Cambodian: written ទឹម, corresponding to the Khmer word meaning ‘to yoke animals’.

variants.name: Timm
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Erwin, Ewald, Helmuth, Irmgard, Ernst, Frieda, Angelika, Arno, Bernhard, Bernhardt, Dieter.

English (Yorkshire and Lincolnshire): from the Middle English pal name Timme, which also gave rise to Timson . It may be a survival of an unrecorded Old English personal name, Tima, of uncertain origin. See also Timms .
North German and Dutch: from a short form of the personal name Dietmar (see Dittmar ).

variants.name: Tims
meanings: Dutch: patronymic from the personal name   Tim   or   Timm  .

nameMeanings.name: Nicolaus
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Nicholas
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

en.lifeSummary: When Nicolaus Mulerch Limbek, til Tørning was born about 1312, in Prussia, Germany, his father, Lensmand Otto Limbek, was 35 and his mother, n.n., was 44. He married Ide Hartvigsdatter Krummedige about 1350. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. He died on 24 January 1373, at the age of 62.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Nicolaus Mulerch Limbek, til Tørning родился около 1312 г. в местности Пруссия, Германия, его отцу по имени Lensmand Otto Limbek было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали n.n., 44. Он женился на женщине по имени Ide Hartvigsdatter Krummedige около 1350 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Он умер 24 января 1373 г. в возрасте 62 лет.
